The warnings in God's word weigh heavily on the wicked, and are a great burden, too heavy for them to bear. The people brought together to destroy Babylon are called God’s consecrated or appointed ones; designed for this service, and made able to do it. They are called God’s mighty ones, because they had their strength from God, and were now to use it for him. They come from a distance. God can make those who are farthest away, and therefore least feared, a scourge and ruin to his enemies.

Here we have the terrible destruction of Babylon by the Medes and Persians. Those who in times of peace were proud, haughty, and terrifying, are completely discouraged when trouble comes. Their faces will be scorched with flame. All comfort and hope will fail. The stars of heaven will not give their light, the sun will be darkened. Such expressions are often used by the prophets to describe the collapse of governments. God will punish them for their wickedness, especially the sin of pride, which brings people low. There will be a general scene of horror. Those who join themselves to Babylon must expect to share her punishments. All that people have, they would give for their lives, but no one’s riches will pay the ransom for his life. Pause here and wonder that people should be so cruel and inhuman, and see how corrupt human nature has become. And that little infants suffer like this shows that there is an original guilt, by which life is forfeited as soon as it begins. The day of the Lord will indeed be terrible with wrath and fierce anger, far beyond all that is stated here. Nor will there be any place for sinners to flee or attempt an escape. But few act as though they believe these things.

Babylon was a noble city; yet it should be completely destroyed. No one will live there. It will be a haunt for wild beasts. All of this has happened. The fate of this proud city is proof of the truth of the Bible, and an example of the coming ruin of New Testament Babylon; a warning to sinners to flee from the wrath that is coming, and it encourages believers to expect victory over every enemy of their souls, and of the church of God. The whole world changes and is subject to decay. Therefore let us be eager to obtain a kingdom which cannot be moved; and in this hope let us hold fast to that grace by which we may serve God acceptably with reverence and godly fear.
